citation: Hara, Yurie. 2015. Alternatives in Cantonese: Disjunctions, Questions and (Un)conditionals. Proceedings of the 20th Amsterdam Colloquium, 177–186.
locator: full paper; distribution table, examples, and reported rating study
claim_supported: Interrogative `ding6`, broader standard disjunction `waak6ze2`, answer-type contrast, casual omission of `hai6` in `ding6hai6`, and rating-study evidence.
limitations: One formal study; exact register, prosody, elliptical alternatives, and all particle combinations require broader speaker evidence.

citation: Huang, Yu-Hsin, One-Soon Her, and Stano Kong. 2025. Revisiting the Taxonomy of Interrogatives in Cantonese. Tsing Hua Journal of Chinese Studies 55(1):157–195. DOI:10.6503/THJCS.202503_55(1).0005.
claim_supported: `定(係)` and `抑或` alternatives; selection rather than truth confirmation; direct and embedded questions; `話/先` tails; proposed `咩`, `究竟`, and `唔通` diagnostics; relationship to A-not-A.
limitations: Taxonomy and LF analysis are theory-specific; most judgments are author examples and negative boundaries require independent replication.

citation: Wong, Anita M.-Y., C. C.-H. Cheung, J. M.-W. Lo, and E. K.-H. Wan. 2022. Grammatical Analysis of Cantonese Samples, in Understanding Development and Disorder in Cantonese Using Language Sample Analysis.
claim_supported: Defines alternative questions as choice questions using `ding6hai6` or short `ding6` and supplies a nominal-alternative example.
limitations: Descriptive framework, not a dedicated acceptability, answerhood, register, or formal-semantic study.

citation: Law, Ann. 2001. A-not-A Questions in Cantonese. UCL Working Papers in Linguistics 13:295–318.
claim_supported: A-not-A affirmative/negative alternatives, non-coordinate derivation argument, and Cantonese reduplication and focus/quantifier boundaries.
limitations: Does not define the full `ding6(hai6)` distribution.

citation: Li, Hui, Shek Kam Tse, Jessie Ming Sin Wong, Eileen Chin Mei Wong, and Shing On Leung. 2013. The development of interrogative forms and functions in early childhood Cantonese. First Language 33(2):168–181. DOI:10.1177/0142723713479422.
claim_supported: Alternative form offering a choice between two alternatives linked by `ding6` or `ding6 hai6`; corpus of 492 Cantonese-speaking children aged 36, 48, and 60 months.
limitations: Child production categories do not establish adult productivity, exact boundaries, particle compatibility, or one syntactic analysis.

citation: 鄭定歐、張勵妍、高石英. 2021. 粵語（香港話）教程（修訂版）. 香港: 三聯書店.
locator: printed pp. 56–60; section 疑問詞「定係」
claim_supported: Bounded-choice `甲定係乙` examples across person, destination, time, manner, and activity alternatives.
limitations: Pedagogical examples support attestation and learner-facing contrasts, not unrestricted productivity or parser promotion.

citation: Canto Span Study Suite complete vocabulary and grammar coverage diagnostics, generated 2026-07-19.

limitations: Parser output has zero independent linguistic evidence weight.
